steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum theorem
steps.content: In any triangle, the sum of the three interior angles equals 180°. We have angle A = 53°, angle B = 90°, and angle C = (k/2)°. Setting up the equation: $$53 + 90 + \frac{k}{2} = 180$$

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content: Simplify the left side: $$143 + \frac{k}{2} = 180$$ Subtract 143 from both sides: $$\frac{k}{2} = 37$$ Multiply both sides by 2: $$k = 74$$ That is the correct answer.
